name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of hands-on experience in materials characterization, analytical method development, and process optimization. Deep expertise in thermal analysis (TGA, DSC), spectroscopy (FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR), particle sizing, and rheology for polymer and nanoparticle systems. Postdoctoral work focuses on biopolymer nanoparticle process development with extensive multi-technique characterization including particle size distribution, morphology, and structure-property relationships. Proven ability to develop robust analytical methods from first principles and apply statistical approaches (DOE, SPC) to complex characterization challenges.

education.details: Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

experience.role: Postdoctoral Researcher — Biopolymer Nanoparticle Process Development
bullets: Lead analytical characterization of biopolymer nanoparticles including particle size distribution (DLS), morphology (TEM/SEM), thermal properties (TGA/DSC), and spectroscopic analysis (FT-IR, UV-Vis).
Develop and optimize process parameters for nanoparticle formulation using DOE approaches, achieving reproducible particle size and size distribution targets.
Establish rheological characterization methods to assess viscosity and flow behavior of polymer solutions during processing.
Apply multivariate analysis to correlate process conditions with material properties, driving data-informed process optimization.
Designed and synthesized novel electrochromic materials with tunable optical and electrochemical properties, requiring extensive multi-technique characterization.
Performed comprehensive thermal analysis (TGA, DSC) to evaluate thermal stability, phase transitions, and decomposition profiles of organic materials.
Conducted spectroscopic analysis (FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, Raman) for structural elucidation, quantification, and real-time process monitoring.
Developed quantitative structure-property relationships using statistical methods and calibration models for predictive materials characterization.
Managed analytical instrumentation including calibration, maintenance, and troubleshooting of spectroscopic and thermal analysis equipment.

skills.Particle & Thermal Analysis: DLS, particle sizing, TGA, DSC, TMA, rheology, viscometry
skills.Microscopy: Optical microscopy, SEM, TEM sample preparation
skills.Data Analysis: DOE, SPC, multivariate analysis, Origin, JMP, MestReNova
skills.Analytical Methods: Method development, validation, calibration, GMP documentation
